text:
$V is partnering with OpenAI to let ChatGPT agents make online purchases pushing AI commerce closer to real transactions.

Visa also highlighted broader AI and stablecoin plans including ~$7B in annualized stablecoin settlement volume.

SK Hynix is preparing to mass-produce its next-generation 375-layer 3D NAND by year-end by converting existing M15 fab lines instead of adding new capacity.

Agentic AI runs on context, retrieval and checkpoints making lower cost-per-bit critical to scaling AI storage demand.

$GOOGL released DiffusionGemma which is an experimental 26B MoE model that uses text diffusion to generate text in blocks instead of one token at a time.

The model delivers up to 4x faster inference and is aimed at speed-critical local AI workflows.

$PLTR CEO Alex Karp was asked about $SPCX and said hes bullish on space when the builder like Elon knows what they're doing.

The SpaceX IPO may become the ultimate test of how public markets price founder-led companies where the asset, mission and operator are all inseparable.

$AMZN says AWS Graviton5 is now generally available making its most powerful CPU chip available to all AWS customers.

AWS says Graviton5 delivers up to 25% better performance and is built for agentic AI workloads that need real-time reasoning and always-on processing.

$TE G2 Austin facility is making real progress bringing T1 closer to producing its own domestic solar cells.

That matters because solar cells are the power-generating core of a solar module and G2 Austin is where the U.S. solar manufacturing buildout starts to look real.

First look at $NVDA photonics co-packaged optics switch with Lambda.

As GB300 NVL72 scales, networking becomes the layer that determines how fast the cluster thinks while co-packaged optics cut power and improve tokens per watt.

$ONDS is launching LADOS which is a unified command-and-control layer that connects sensors, drones, robots and effectors for autonomous defense missions. 

Paired with SkyWeaver (Ondas $PLTR powered AI layer) LADOS helps move the company from individual drone products toward a broader systems-of-systems defense platform.

$HOOD JUST SAW A $20M INSIDER BUY

Director Meyer Malka bought another 250K shares at ~$81 bringing his stake to 8M shares.

He has now purchased more than $50M of $HOOD stock over the past week.

$AMZN expanded its Supply Chain Services business by rolling out less-than-truckload freight shipping across the U.S.

Businesses can now use Amazon to ship palletized freight to a wide range of destinations instead of only Amazon facilities.

$META is partnering with Reliance to lease a 168MW AI data center in India powered by renewable energy.

The deal also supports nearly 1GW of new clean energy projects as Meta expands its AI infrastructure footprint in India.

OpenAI is reportedly in talks to lease a proposed 10GW AI data center campus in Ohio that could cost over $500B to build.

$NVDA would supply the hardware and provide financial guarantees while OpenAI would operate the site under a 20-year lease

For 2027, TrendForce now projects the global memory market to reach $1.28 trillion, up 44% YoY, with DRAM revenue forecast to surpass $900 billion. 

This would be more than 5X larger than the memory market in 2025, at $224.7 billion.

Trade SpaceX $SPCX with me LIVE:

t3live.com/7

📺 HOW TO TRADE A TACTICAL MARKET
+ $SPCX IPO & TRADES IN $ASTS / $RKLB

In this Short video, I explain how my approach changes when the market shifts from a trending environment to a tactical one.

After $SPY broke below key levels and failed to reclaim resistance, I aggressively reduced risk, moved largely to cash in my swing accounts, and focused on short-term opportunities rather than portfolio-style investing.

*

I know many traders successfully navigated the recent weakness by shorting failed rallies, buying $SPY and $SOXX puts, and selling into strength rather than chasing upside. 

While volatility has increased and market leadership has deteriorated, I do not believe you need to aggressively press shorts after the recent decline. 

Instead, cover portions of profitable short positions, sell premium, and watch for stocks that can turn green early and offer tactical trading opportunities.

*

With the SpaceX IPO set for tomorrow, I see space stocks like $ASTS and $RKLB as potential candidates for red-to-green trades today.

I would trade both names tactically, taking profits into resistance and avoiding oversized exposure. 

When the broader market is below key moving averages, stocks should be treated as trades rather than long-term commitments. 

In a tactical tape, the goal is to “love it and leave it.”

*

I also share my framework ("The Art Of The First Day") for trading the upcoming SpaceX IPO. 

Rather than chasing the excitement surrounding a high-profile IPO, I focus on supply and demand. I want to see where the stock opens, whether it can hold above its opening price, and how it trades during the first 15 minutes. 

Many modern IPOs stay private for too long, allowing early investors and insiders to capture most of the gains before public investors get involved.

So, while I believe SpaceX could eventually become an outstanding company and potentially a strong long-term investment, I am cautious that day one and week one are often not the best times to get involved. 

I prefer to let institutions accumulate shares, allow early investors to distribute stock, and wait for a proper 3- to 6-month base before considering a larger investment position.

*

So, different market environments require different tactics. 

When the market is trending, investors can build positions and let them work. 

When the market becomes tactical and volatile, capital preservation, flexibility, and disciplined execution become the priorities.

*

SpaceX reported a $4.9B net loss in 2025.

The losses are driven by heavy investment across rockets, satellites, AI infrastructure, and GPU-intensive compute.

SpaceX management describes this as a deliberate, front-loaded investment to build long-term leadership across space, connectivity, and AI.

To us, this is not new. We've seen this same playbook among public mega-cap tech companies.

For Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Broadcom, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Oracle, total capex reached about 427 billion dollars in 2025 and is projected to rise roughly 30% to about 562 billion dollars in 2026. *Data from RBC Wealth Management as of 2/3/2026.
